summary: Frontend-only testing of Superhero HQ app. All core features tested: Planning Mode, quest selection, Launch Mission, Active Tracking Mode, HQ Visit Mode, Volt mascot, and custom quest form. One critical bug found with Welcome Screen bypassed due to React StrictMode side effect.

ui_bugs.issue: Welcome Screen never shows on fresh load even after localStorage.clear(). App starts directly in Planning Mode.

issues: Streak counter shows '0 DAY STREAK' text without a clear numeric badge - acceptable but could be more prominent

action_items: Fix Welcome Screen bypass bug: React StrictMode causes save useEffect to write appView='planning' to localStorage on first render, then load useEffect on second StrictMode run reads 'planning' and dispatches LOAD_STATE, skipping welcome screen. Fix: (a) use a separate 'hasSeenWelcome' flag instead of relying on appView in localStorage, OR (b) in the save effect, only save to localStorage if the state has changed from initialState, OR (c) remove React.StrictMode from index.js (not recommended). Most robust fix: store a 'welcomeShown' boolean in localStorage separately; only skip welcome if that flag is true.
critical_code_review_comments: Welcome Screen bypass RCA: AppContext.js line 216 converts 'welcome' to 'planning' when saving to localStorage (toSave.appView = appView === 'welcome' ? 'planning' : appView). In React.StrictMode (index.js line 8), effects run twice. On the second run, the load effect reads localStorage which already has appView='planning' (written by save effect on first run) and dispatches LOAD_STATE, bypassing welcome screen.
AppContext.js line 90: LOAD_STATE handler also converts 'welcome' to 'planning', meaning even if somehow welcome is in localStorage it gets converted.
FREQ_COLORS object in CommandCenter.jsx line 14 has duplicate key 'Quarterly' - second one overrides first. Minor issue but worth noting.
seed_data_creation: None - pure localStorage app with built-in blueprint data
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is a pure frontend localStorage app. To test welcome screen, the StrictMode bug needs fixing first. All other features work: planning mode, floor/room accordions, quest selection, EP counter, launch mission, tracking mode with progress ring/streak/buffers/task cards, HQ Visit Mode, Volt mascot, custom quest form. The data-testid attribute for ENTER HQ button is 'enter-hq-button' (not 'enter-hq-btn'). Floor accordions don't have data-testid but room accordions do: data-testid='room-{id}-accordion'.

rca_of_issue.root_cause: React.StrictMode in index.js causes all useEffects to mount, unmount, and remount. The save useEffect (defined second in AppContext.js) converts appView='welcome' to 'planning' when writing to localStorage. When effects remount (StrictMode second pass), the load useEffect reads this 'planning' value from localStorage and dispatches LOAD_STATE({appView:'planning'}), overriding the initial 'welcome' state before the component renders the welcome screen.
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Clear localStorage 2. Load app 3. App shows Planning Mode instead of Welcome Screen
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Use a dedicated 'hasSeenWelcome' boolean flag in localStorage, only set when user clicks ENTER HQ. Check this flag in AppContext to determine initial view instead of saving/restoring appView.
